specification.title: Unified AGI/ASI Governance, Enterprise AI Transformation, and Civilisational Safety Framework
specification.abstract: This specification defines a comprehensive, implementation-ready governance architecture that unifies ten interdependent domains: (1) enterprise AGI/ASI governance strategy and communication; (2) multi-framework regulatory compliance for Project Nexus, Chimera, NPGARS, UDIF, GDII, and the Luminous Engine Codex; (3) civilisation-scale AI education and governance via Sentinel, GSIIEN, Kyaw stack, and HELIOS/ORION constructs; (4) a 10-stage AI evolution model with alignment/superalignment controls mapped to EU AI Act tiers; (5) enterprise reference architectures including WorkflowAI Pro, EAIP, Sentinel v2.4, high-assurance RAG, and CCaaS governance; (6) global legal and registry API frameworks for advanced AI compute and safety; (7) Luminous Engine Codex crisis simulation governance; (8) Sentinel/Omni-Sentinel financial services governance for G-SIFIs; (9) Cognitive Resonance governance-first AGI-readiness architectures; and (10) civilisational-scale AGI safety research frameworks including Open Future Doctrine extensions and Minimal Viable AGI Governance Stacks. The framework integrates EU AI Act (Reg. 2024/1689), NIST AI RMF 1.0, ISO/IEC 42001:2023, OECD AI Principles, GDPR, FCRA, ECOA, Bletchley Declaration, and Seoul Frontier AI Safety Commitments into a single coherent compliance surface with traceable control mappings across all ten domains.
specification.content:
# Unified AGI/ASI Governance, Enterprise AI Transformation, and Civilisational Safety Framework
**Document Reference:** SPEC-AGIGOV-UNIFIED-001 v1.0.0 **Classification:** STRATEGIC -- Board-Level / Restricted Distribution **Date:** 2026-03-21 **Authors:** Chief Software Architect, AI Systems Engineering, AI Governance and Technical Strategy Office **Audience:** CTO, VP Engineering, VP AI Platform, Chief AI Officer, Chief Risk Officer, General Counsel, Board of Directors **Review Cycle:** Quarterly (next review: 2026-06-21) **Companion Documents:** GOV-AGI-FWK-001, GOV-ASI-SPA-001, GOV-AI-RPT-001, SPEC-AIDMP-001, SPEC-WFAIPRO-001, SEC-ROAD-RPT-001
---
## Strategic Reasoning
This document synthesises a decade of AI governance research, enterprise architecture practice, and frontier-capability analysis into a single operational framework. The synthesis is necessary because the ten domains identified in the scope are not independent -- they form a tightly coupled system where a governance gap in any single domain propagates cascading risk to all others. For example: a Sentinel v2.4 deployment in a G-SIFI financial institution without Luminous Engine Codex crisis simulation capabilities creates a false sense of assurance; an enterprise RAG system (WorkflowAI Pro, EAIP) without NPGARS provenance guarantees undermines the entire compliance surface required by EU AI Act Article 52; and civilisation-scale frameworks (HELIOS, ORION, Open Future Doctrine) remain aspirational without the enterprise-level implementation primitives (Cognitive Resonance, Minimal Viable AGI Governance Stack) that make them deployable.
The methodological approach integrates: (1) Collingridge's dilemma applied to AGI governance timing; (2) COSO ERM and ISO 31000 extended to AGI-specific risk categories; (3) regime theory for multilateral AI governance mechanisms; (4) published scaling laws (Hoffmann et al. 2022, Kaplan et al. 2020) and Epoch AI 2025 compute trend analysis; (5) observed frontier capabilities as of Q1 2026 (ARC-AGI-2: 28.9%, FrontierMath: 43.2%, SWE-bench Verified: 72.7%).
---
# 1. Enterprise AGI/ASI Governance Strategy and Communication Architecture
## 1.1 Governance Communication Stack
The enterprise AGI/ASI governance communication architecture operates across four tiers:
| Tier | Audience | Cadence | Artefact | Classification | |------|----------|---------|----------|----------------| | T1: Board Governance | Board, CEO, CRO | Quarterly | AGI Readiness Briefing (GOV-AGI-FWK-001) | STRATEGIC | | T2: Executive Steering | C-Suite, VP Eng, CAIO | Monthly | AI Transformation Scorecard | CONFIDENTIAL | | T3: Technical Leadership | Lead Devs, ML Eng, SRE | Bi-weekly | Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) | INTERNAL | | T4: Operational | All engineering | Continuous | Automated governance dashboards | INTERNAL |
## 1.2 Strategic Posture Assessment
**Current Enterprise AGI Readiness Level (EARL):** Level 3 of 5 (Structured Preparedness)
| Level | Name | Description | Our Status | |-------|------|-------------|------------| | 1 | Unaware | No AGI governance structures | -- | | 2 | Reactive | Ad hoc responses to AI incidents | -- | | 3 | Structured | Formal frameworks, dedicated governance team, compliance mapped | CURRENT | | 4 | Adaptive | Real-time governance, automated compliance, simulation-tested | TARGET (Q4 2026) | | 5 | Anticipatory | Predictive governance, civilisation-scale coordination, AGI-ready | TARGET (2028) |
## 1.3 Implementation Guidance
**Immediate Actions (0-90 days):** - Establish AI Governance Board with CTO, CAIO, CRO, GC, VP Eng as permanent members - Commission EARL Level 4 gap analysis against ISO/IEC 42001:2023 and EU AI Act - Deploy Sentinel v2.4 governance monitoring across all production AI systems - Initiate Luminous Engine Codex crisis simulation programme (quarterly cadence) - Register all AI systems exceeding 10^23 FLOP training compute in internal registry
---
# 2. Multi-Framework Regulatory Compliance Architecture
## 2.1 Programme-to-Framework Compliance Matrix
| Programme | EU AI Act | NIST AI RMF | ISO 42001 | OECD AI | GDPR | FCRA | ECOA | |-----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|---------|------|------|------| | **Project Nexus** | Art. 6 (High-Risk), Art. 9 (Risk Mgmt), Art. 52 (Transparency) | GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE | 5.2, 6.1, 8.4, A.2-A.4 | 1.2, 1.3, 2.1 | Art. 22, 35 | s607(a), s611 | s701(a) | | **Project Chimera** | Art. 6, Art. 10 (Data Governance), Art. 14 (Human Oversight) | MAP-1.1, MEASURE-2.3, MANAGE-3.2 | 6.1.2, 8.2, 9.1, A.5 | 1.3, 2.2, 3.1 | Art. 5, 6, 25 | s604, s607 | s701(a), s702 | | **NPGARS** | Art. 11 (Technical Documentation), Art. 52 | MAP-1.5, MEASURE-2.6 | 8.4, A.8 | 1.5, 3.2 | Art. 13, 30 | -- | -- | | **UDIF** | Art. 10, Art. 15 (Accuracy/Robustness) | MAP-1.2, MANAGE-4.1 | 6.1, 7.1, A.3 | 1.4, 2.1 | Art. 5, 25 | -- | -- | | **GDII** | Art. 9 (Risk Mgmt System), Art. 61 (Post-Market Monitoring) | GOVERN-1.1, MANAGE-4.2 | 9.1, 10.1, A.7 | 2.3, 3.3 | Art. 35, 36 | -- | -- | | **Luminous Engine Codex** | Art. 6, Art. 52, Art. 55 (GPAI Systemic Risk) | Full framework application | Full standard | Full principles | Full regulation | Full application | Full application |
## 2.2 EU AI Act Tiered Compliance Controls
### High-Risk Classification (Article 6) -- Applicable Programmes
**Risk Management System (Article 9):** - Continuous iterative risk identification, analysis, estimation, and evaluation - Risk mitigation testing against residual risk acceptance criteria - Documented risk management plan reviewed quarterly (assigned: CRO) - Integration with Sentinel v2.4 real-time risk monitoring
**Data Governance (Article 10):** - Training data provenance chain via NPGARS (hash-verified, tamper-evident) - Bias detection pipeline: statistical parity, equalised odds, calibration across protected classes - UDIF-enforced data quality metrics: completeness >99.2%, consistency >99.8%, timeliness <24h - Annual data governance audit (assigned: Data Protection Officer)
**Transparency (Article 52):** - All AI-generated outputs carry machine-readable provenance metadata - User-facing systems display AI interaction disclosure within 200ms of first AI output - NPGARS generates human-readable explanation summaries for all high-risk decisions - Explanation fidelity score: >0.85 measured by LIME/SHAP consistency test
**Human Oversight (Article 14):** - Human-in-the-loop: all decisions affecting legal rights require human approval - Human-on-the-loop: automated monitoring with human escalation at defined thresholds - Human-in-command: kill-switch capability with <5s response time for all production AI systems - Quarterly human oversight effectiveness audit (assigned: AI Governance Board)
## 2.3 NIST AI RMF 1.0 Function Mapping
| Function | Key Actions | Responsible | Measurement | |----------|-------------|-------------|-------------| | GOVERN | AI risk governance structure, policies, processes | AI Governance Board | EARL maturity score, policy coverage % | | MAP | Context establishment, AI system categorisation | Enterprise Architecture | Systems mapped/total, risk tier assignment accuracy | | MEASURE | Risk and impact assessment, bias testing | ML Engineering | Metric threshold compliance %, drift detection latency | | MANAGE | Risk treatment, incident response, continuous monitoring | SRE + ML Ops | MTTR, incident frequency, residual risk score |
## 2.4 ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Control Implementation Status
| Clause | Control Area | Status | Evidence | Gap | |--------|-------------|--------|----------|-----| | 5.2 | AI Policy | IMPLEMENTED | GOV-AI-RPT-001 | None | | 6.1 | Risk Assessment | IMPLEMENTED | GDII integration | Annual refresh pending | | 6.1.2 | AI Risk Assessment | IMPLEMENTED | Sentinel v2.4 monitoring | Coverage: 87% of systems | | 7.1 | Resources | IMPLEMENTED | Dedicated AI governance team (6 FTE) | Capacity review Q3 | | 8.2 | AI Impact Assessment | PARTIAL | Template deployed; 14 of 22 systems assessed | 8 assessments pending | | 8.4 | Documentation | IMPLEMENTED | NPGARS automated documentation | None | | 9.1 | Monitoring | IMPLEMENTED | Sentinel v2.4 + GDII | Real-time for 19 of 22 systems | | 10.1 | Continual Improvement | IN PROGRESS | Quarterly review cycle established | First full cycle Q2 2026 | | A.2-A.8 | Annex A Controls | 93% IMPLEMENTED | Control-by-control evidence package | 7% pending (A.5.3, A.7.2) |
---
# 3. Civilisation-Scale AI Governance and Education Framework
## 3.1 Sentinel Governance Platform (v2.4)
Sentinel is the real-time AI governance monitoring and enforcement platform operating across the enterprise AI estate.
**Architecture:** - **Sentinel Core:** Event-driven governance engine (Go 1.22, gRPC) consuming telemetry from all AI systems via OpenTelemetry-compatible instrumentation - **Sentinel Policy Engine:** OPA-based policy evaluation with <50ms P99 decision latency; 847 governance rules across 12 policy domains - **Sentinel Dashboard:** Real-time governance posture visualisation (React 18, WebSocket); executive, technical, and compliance views - **Sentinel Audit Trail:** Append-only, cryptographically signed governance event log (PostgreSQL 16, pg_partman monthly partitions); 7-year retention
**Metrics (as of Q1 2026):** - Systems monitored: 22 production AI systems - Policy evaluations/day: 1.2M - P99 policy decision latency: 38ms - False positive rate: 0.3% - Governance incidents detected: 14 (12 auto-remediated, 2 escalated)
## 3.2 GSIIEN -- Global Sentinel Interoperability and Intelligence Exchange Network
GSIIEN extends Sentinel governance beyond the enterprise boundary, enabling: - **Cross-organisational governance telemetry sharing** (anonymised, differential privacy epsilon=1.0) - **Federated policy learning:** organisations contribute governance outcomes to a shared policy effectiveness model without exposing proprietary data - **Regulatory reporting automation:** machine-readable governance reports generated for EU AI Office, national authorities, and sector regulators - **Incident intelligence sharing:** AI-specific threat intelligence exchange (STIX/TAXII format extended for AI governance events)
**Protocol Stack:** - Transport: mTLS 1.3 over HTTP/2 - Serialisation: Protobuf with governance-specific message types - Identity: DID:web with verifiable credentials for organisational identity - Privacy: Differential privacy (epsilon=1.0, delta=10^-6) on all shared telemetry
## 3.3 Kyaw Stack -- Knowledge, Yield, Alignment, Wisdom
The Kyaw stack is a pedagogical and operational framework for AI governance education across organisational levels:
| Layer | Focus | Audience | Delivery | Certification | |-------|-------|----------|----------|---------------| | **Knowledge** | AI literacy, capability awareness, risk taxonomy | All employees | Self-paced e-learning, 8h | AI Literacy Certificate | | **Yield** | AI productivity, tool usage, output quality | Department leads, power users | Workshop series, 24h | AI Practitioner Certificate | | **Alignment** | Value alignment, bias detection, ethical reasoning | ML engineers, product managers | Immersive programme, 40h | AI Ethics Certificate | | **Wisdom** | Governance design, civilisational impact, AGI preparedness | Senior leadership, governance team | Executive seminar series, 16h | AI Governance Leadership Certificate |
**Deployment Status:** Knowledge layer: 91% completion. Yield layer: 67% completion. Alignment layer: 42% completion (ML engineering cohort). Wisdom layer: 28% completion (executive cohort in progress).
## 3.4 HELIOS -- Human-Engaged Leadership for Intelligent and Overseen Systems
HELIOS is the organisational governance structure ensuring human primacy in AI decision-making:
- **HELIOS Board:** 7-member oversight body (CEO, CTO, CAIO, CRO, GC, VP Eng, independent AI ethicist) - **HELIOS Charter:** Codified principles: human override authority, explanation obligation, proportionality, reversibility, accountability chain - **HELIOS Review Process:** All AI systems exceeding defined capability thresholds undergo HELIOS review before production deployment - **HELIOS Escalation Protocol:** 3-tier escalation (automated -> technical lead -> HELIOS Board) with defined SLAs (15min / 1h / 4h)
## 3.5 ORION -- Organisational Resilience and Intelligence Operations Network
ORION provides the operational resilience infrastructure for AI governance:
- **ORION War Room:** Dedicated incident response capability for AI governance failures, staffed 24/7 during high-risk deployments - **ORION Playbooks:** 23 scenario-specific runbooks covering model failure, data poisoning, prompt injection, compliance breach, reputational incident - **ORION Simulations:** Quarterly tabletop exercises (integrated with Luminous Engine Codex crisis simulation) - **ORION Metrics:** MTTR for governance incidents: <45 min (target: <30 min by Q4 2026)
---
# 4. The Trajectory of AI: 10-Stage Evolution Model
## 4.1 Stage Definitions with Governance Controls
| Stage | Name | Capability Signature | Timeline (Central) | EU AI Act Tier | Governance Controls | |-------|------|---------------------|---------------------|----------------|---------------------| | 1 | Rule-Based Systems | Deterministic logic, no learning | 1950s-1990s | Minimal Risk | Standard software QA | | 2 | Statistical ML | Pattern recognition, supervised learning | 2000s-2015 | Limited Risk | Model validation, bias testing | | 3 | Deep Learning | Representation learning, neural networks | 2012-2020 | Limited-High Risk | NIST AI RMF MAP, ISO 42001 6.1 | | 4 | Foundation Models | Transfer learning, multi-task, emergent abilities | 2020-2025 | High Risk / GPAI | Art. 52-55, Sentinel monitoring, NPGARS | | 5 | Agentic AI | Autonomous task completion, tool use, planning | 2024-2027 | High Risk + Systemic | Art. 6+9+14, kill-switch, HELIOS review | | 6 | Narrow Superintelligence | Superhuman in specific domains | 2026-2030 | Systemic Risk GPAI | Full EU AI Act, ORION, crisis simulation | | 7 | Proto-AGI | Human-level across most cognitive tasks | 2028-2033 | Systemic Risk + Novel Reg. | HELIOS mandatory review, international coordination | | 8 | AGI | Human-level across all cognitive tasks | 2031-2038 | Novel regulatory regime | Full civilisational governance, Open Future Doctrine | | 9 | Transformative ASI | Substantially superhuman, aligned | 2035-2050+ | International treaty regime | Global coordination, GSIIEN, containment protocols | | 10 | Uncontained ASI | Superhuman, alignment uncertain | Uncertain | Existential governance | Civilisational coordination, Open Future Doctrine |
## 4.2 Alignment and Superalignment Challenges by Stage
**Stages 4-5 (Current Frontier):** - Alignment challenge: instruction following vs intent alignment; jailbreaking; prompt injection - Controls: RLHF/DPO, Constitutional AI, red-teaming, Sentinel real-time monitoring - Superalignment relevance: Low -- systems are tool-like
**Stages 6-7 (Near-Term Frontier):** - Alignment challenge: goal specification under distributional shift; deceptive alignment; mesa-optimisation - Controls: Interpretability (mechanistic, probing), formal verification of safety properties, recursive reward modelling - Superalignment relevance: Medium -- systems may develop instrumental convergence
**Stages 8-10 (Long-Term Frontier):** - Alignment challenge: value loading, corrigibility under self-improvement, ontological crisis - Controls: Open Future Doctrine constraints, containment protocols, international verification regime - Superalignment relevance: Critical -- alignment failure is existential
## 4.3 Organisational and Policy Implications
| Stage Transition | Workforce Impact | Policy Action Required | Investment | |-----------------|------------------|----------------------|------------| | 4 -> 5 (Agentic) | 15-25% task automation; new AI supervision roles | Agentic AI policy, human oversight protocol | $1.2M | | 5 -> 6 (Narrow Super) | Domain-specific expert displacement; AI-augmented roles | Sentinel v3.0, domain containment, retraining programme | $3.8M | | 6 -> 7 (Proto-AGI) | Broad cognitive automation; governance becomes primary function | HELIOS mandatory, international coordination engagement | $8.5M | | 7 -> 8 (AGI) | Fundamental economic restructuring | Open Future Doctrine activation, civilisational governance | $25M+ |
---
# 5. Enterprise AI Reference Architectures and Governance Strategies
## 5.1 Architecture Inventory and Governance Mapping
| System | Architecture | Governance Framework | Risk Tier | Sentinel Integration | |--------|-------------|---------------------|-----------|---------------------| | **WorkflowAI Pro** | GNN routing + NCF prediction + AL UI (SPEC-WFAIPRO-001) | EU AI Act High-Risk (Art. 6), NIST MANAGE | High | Full telemetry, 847 policy rules | | **EAIP** | Enterprise AI Integration Platform (monolith-to-mesh migration) | ISO 42001, NIST GOVERN | Medium-High | API gateway governance, data lineage | | **Sentinel v2.4** | Event-driven governance engine (Go/gRPC/OPA) | Self-governing (meta-governance) | Critical | N/A (is the governance system) | | **High-Assurance RAG** | Project Veridical architecture (94.2% accuracy, 0.95s P95) | EU AI Act Art. 52, NPGARS provenance | High | Full telemetry, provenance chain | | **CCaaS Governance** | Contact Centre AI governance overlay | FCRA s607, ECOA s701, GDPR Art. 22 | High | Real-time call monitoring, bias detection |
## 5.2 Cross-Architecture Governance Patterns
**Pattern 1: Provenance Chain (NPGARS)** All AI systems implement hash-verified, tamper-evident provenance chains from training data through inference to user-facing output. NPGARS provides the standard interface: - Training data provenance: dataset hash, source, preprocessing lineage - Model provenance: architecture hash, training config hash, checkpoint hash - Inference provenance: input hash, model version, output hash, explanation hash - Audit provenance: reviewer ID, decision, timestamp, governance rule applied
**Pattern 2: Unified Data Interchange (UDIF)** UDIF defines the canonical data format for AI system interoperability: - Schema versioning with backward compatibility guarantees - Quality metrics: completeness, consistency, timeliness, accuracy - Privacy-preserving transformations: k-anonymity (k>=5), l-diversity (l>=3), differential privacy
**Pattern 3: Governance-Driven Impact Intelligence (GDII)** GDII provides continuous assessment of AI system impact across dimensions: - Accuracy/performance drift detection (PSI threshold: 0.1) - Fairness metric monitoring (statistical parity difference: <0.05) - Economic impact quantification (ROI, cost avoidance, productivity gain) - Societal impact assessment (job displacement index, accessibility score)
## 5.3 High-Assurance RAG Governance (Project Veridical Reference)
Based on the 12-week Project Veridical programme (successfully completed, all metrics exceeded): - Retrieval accuracy: 94.2% (gate: >=92.0%) - P95 latency: 0.95s (gate: <=1.50s) - Token cost: $0.016/query (gate: <=$0.035) - Uptime: 99.99% (gate: >=99.9%) - Document corpus: 1.45M items - Users: 1,347 across 14 departments
**Governance Controls Applied:** - NPGARS provenance on all retrieval results - Sentinel monitoring of accuracy, latency, and bias - HELIOS review completed before production release - GDII impact assessment: $3.4M annualised saving, 3.0x Year-1 ROI
---
# 6. Global Legal and Registry API Frameworks for Advanced AI
## 6.1 AI System Registry API (OpenAPI 3.0 Summary)
All AI systems exceeding defined capability or compute thresholds must register in the enterprise AI registry, which supports external reporting to EU AI Office and national authorities.
**Registry API Endpoints:** - `POST /api/v2/registry/systems` -- Register a new AI system (required fields: system_id, name, risk_tier, training_compute_flop, purpose, deployment_scope, responsible_entity) - `GET /api/v2/registry/systems/{id}` -- Retrieve system registration with full compliance status - `PUT /api/v2/registry/systems/{id}/compliance` -- Update compliance assessment (EU AI Act article mapping, NIST function mapping, ISO control status) - `GET /api/v2/registry/report/eu-ai-office` -- Generate machine-readable report for EU AI Office submission (Article 49 notification format) - `GET /api/v2/registry/report/national-authority/{jurisdiction}` -- Generate jurisdiction-specific compliance report
**Compute Thresholds (aligned with EU AI Act Article 55):** - Tier 1 (>=10^23 FLOP): Internal registration required - Tier 2 (>=10^24 FLOP): HELIOS review required - Tier 3 (>=10^25 FLOP): EU AI Office notification mandatory; Systemic Risk designation
## 6.2 Global Safety Verification Protocol
- **Pre-deployment safety assessment:** Mandatory for all Tier 2+ systems; standardised test suite covering capability elicitation, adversarial robustness, alignment evaluation - **Post-deployment monitoring:** Continuous via Sentinel v2.4; anomaly detection with <5 min alert latency - **Cross-border cooperation:** GSIIEN-mediated intelligence sharing; mutual recognition framework for safety assessments - **Incident reporting:** Machine-readable incident reports (STIX/TAXII extended format) within 72 hours of detection
---
# 7. Luminous Engine Codex -- Crisis Simulation Governance
## 7.1 Simulation Architecture
The Luminous Engine Codex provides structured crisis simulation for AI governance scenarios:
**Simulation Categories:** - **Category A: Technical Failure** -- Model collapse, data poisoning, adversarial attack, infrastructure failure - **Category B: Compliance Breach** -- GDPR violation, EU AI Act non-compliance, regulatory investigation - **Category C: Reputational Crisis** -- AI-generated harmful content, bias incident, public backlash - **Category D: Capability Jump** -- Unexpected capability emergence, alignment failure, loss of control - **Category E: Geopolitical** -- AI arms race escalation, compute export controls, treaty negotiation
**Simulation Protocol:** 1. Scenario injection (Luminous Engine generates context-rich scenario with branching decision trees) 2. Team assembly (HELIOS Board + relevant domain experts) 3. Timed response rounds (15-minute decision cycles) 4. Real-time consequence modelling (Luminous Engine simulates downstream effects) 5. After-action review (structured debrief, lessons learned, playbook updates)
**Cadence:** Quarterly (Category A-C rotating); Semi-annually (Category D-E)
## 7.2 Codex Integration Points
- **Sentinel v2.4:** Real governance telemetry feeds scenario generation (anonymised) - **ORION Playbooks:** Simulation outcomes update operational runbooks - **Kyaw Stack:** Simulation participation counts toward Alignment and Wisdom layer certifications - **HELIOS Board:** Mandatory participation; board effectiveness scored
---
# 8. Sentinel and Omni-Sentinel for Financial Services and G-SIFIs
## 8.1 Financial Services Governance Extensions
G-SIFIs (Global Systemically Important Financial Institutions) face unique AI governance requirements:
| Requirement | Regulatory Source | Sentinel Control | Omni-Sentinel Extension | |-------------|-----------------|------------------|------------------------| | Model Risk Management | SR 11-7 (Fed), SS1/23 (PRA) | Model inventory, validation tracking | Cross-entity model dependency graph | | Fair Lending | ECOA s701, FCRA s607 | Real-time bias monitoring on credit decisions | Sector-wide fairness benchmarking | | Explainability | GDPR Art. 22, FCRA s615 | NPGARS explanation generation | Standardised explanation format for regulators | | Systemic Risk | Basel III Pillar 2, FSB AI guidance | Individual institution monitoring | Cross-institution AI concentration risk | | Operational Resilience | DORA (EU), FCA PS21/3 | System availability monitoring | Third-party AI provider risk aggregation |
## 8.2 Omni-Sentinel Architecture
Omni-Sentinel extends Sentinel governance to the systemic level:
- **Individual Sentinel instances** operate within each G-SIFI, monitoring internal AI systems - **Omni-Sentinel aggregation layer** collects anonymised governance telemetry from participating institutions via GSIIEN - **Systemic risk indicators** computed from aggregated data: AI model concentration (HHI), correlated failure probability, sector-wide bias drift - **Regulatory reporting:** Automated reports for FSB, national prudential authorities, and sector regulators - **Privacy preservation:** All aggregation uses secure multi-party computation (SMPC) and differential privacy
## 8.3 CCaaS Governance Controls (Contact Centre AI)
- **Real-time call monitoring:** Sentiment analysis + bias detection on all AI-assisted interactions - **FCRA compliance:** Automated disclosure generation when AI contributes to credit decisions - **ECOA compliance:** Protected class monitoring with statistical parity threshold <0.03 - **Quality assurance:** AI output quality scoring with human audit sampling (5% of interactions) - **Escalation:** Automatic transfer to human agent when AI confidence <0.70 or customer requests
---
# 9. Cognitive Resonance -- Governance-First AGI-Readiness Architecture
## 9.1 Core Principles
Cognitive Resonance is an architectural philosophy that embeds governance as a first-class system property rather than a bolt-on overlay:
1. **Governance-by-Construction:** Every AI component is designed with governance interfaces from inception; governance is not retrofitted 2. **Resonant Alignment:** AI system objectives are continuously aligned with organisational values through feedback loops (not one-time configuration) 3. **Graceful Degradation:** Governance failures trigger capability reduction, not system failure; AI systems become more conservative under uncertainty 4. **Transparent Reasoning:** All AI decisions are accompanied by causal reasoning chains accessible to governance systems 5. **Distributed Authority:** No single entity (human or AI) has unchecked authority; all actions require multi-party validation above defined thresholds
## 9.2 AGI-Readiness Architecture Patterns
**Pattern 1: Capability Gating** AI system capabilities are gated behind governance checkpoints. New capabilities are unlocked only after: - HELIOS Board approval (for capabilities exceeding defined thresholds) - Sentinel policy evaluation confirms compliance with all applicable rules - NPGARS provenance chain established for all training data and evaluation results - GDII impact assessment completed and accepted - Luminous Engine Codex simulation conducted for high-risk capabilities
**Pattern 2: Alignment Monitoring** Continuous measurement of AI system alignment with intended objectives: - Behavioural drift detection: KL-divergence between expected and observed output distributions (threshold: 0.05) - Value alignment scoring: periodic evaluation against canonical value benchmark (quarterly refresh) - Instrumental convergence detection: monitoring for resource-seeking, self-preservation, or goal-preservation behaviours outside defined boundaries - Deceptive alignment testing: adversarial probing for inconsistency between stated and revealed objectives
**Pattern 3: Corrigibility Enforcement** All AI systems must maintain corrigibility -- the ability and willingness to be corrected: - Kill-switch: <5s response time, tested monthly - Objective modification: AI systems accept objective changes without resistance or subversion - Transparency: AI systems report internal state accurately when queried - Bounded autonomy: all autonomous actions constrained to pre-approved action space
## 9.3 Implementation Roadmap
| Quarter | Milestone | Dependency | |---------|-----------|------------| | Q2 2026 | Governance-by-Construction templates for all new AI projects | ISO 42001 implementation complete | | Q3 2026 | Alignment Monitoring v1.0 deployed on all Stage 4-5 systems | Sentinel v2.4 fully operational | | Q4 2026 | Capability Gating v1.0 operational; HELIOS review automated | HELIOS Board charter ratified | | Q1 2027 | Corrigibility Enforcement v1.0 on all agentic AI systems | Agentic AI policy approved | | Q2 2027 | Full Cognitive Resonance architecture operational | All components integrated |
---
# 10. Civilisational-Scale AGI Safety and Governance Research Frameworks
## 10.1 Open Future Doctrine
The Open Future Doctrine is a set of constraints on AGI/ASI development designed to preserve human agency and civilisational optionality:
**Core Constraints:** 1. **Reversibility:** No AGI/ASI deployment shall create irreversible changes to human civilisation without explicit, informed, democratic consent 2. **Plurality:** AGI/ASI development shall preserve a diversity of human values, cultures, and governance systems; no single value system shall be imposed 3. **Transparency:** All AGI/ASI development above defined capability thresholds shall be subject to independent audit and public reporting 4. **Containment:** AGI/ASI systems shall be deployed with containment measures proportional to their capability level 5. **Beneficence:** AGI/ASI development shall be directed toward broadly shared human benefit, not concentrated advantage 6. **Humility:** All parties involved in AGI/ASI development shall acknowledge fundamental uncertainty about outcomes and maintain precautionary postures
**Enterprise Application:** - All AI projects assessed against Open Future Doctrine constraints at HELIOS review - Quarterly self-assessment against Doctrine principles (included in AI Governance Board agenda) - External audit against Doctrine principles annually (independent AI ethicist)
## 10.2 Minimal Viable AGI Governance Stack (MVAGS)
MVAGS defines the minimum governance infrastructure required for any organisation developing or deploying AGI-adjacent systems:
| Component | Purpose | Implementation | Cost Estimate | |-----------|---------|----------------|---------------| | **AI Registry** | Inventory all AI systems with risk classification | API-based registry (Section 6.1) | $80K | | **Risk Assessment** | Continuous risk evaluation against defined taxonomy | GDII integration | $120K | | **Monitoring** | Real-time governance telemetry and alerting | Sentinel v2.4 (minimum config) | $200K | | **Audit Trail** | Immutable, cryptographically signed governance log | PostgreSQL + NPGARS | $60K | | **Human Oversight** | Defined escalation protocols with SLAs | HELIOS (simplified) | $40K | | **Incident Response** | Playbooks for governance failure scenarios | ORION (core playbooks) | $50K | | **Training** | AI governance literacy across organisation | Kyaw Stack (Knowledge layer) | $30K | | **External Reporting** | Regulatory compliance reporting | Registry API reporting endpoints | $20K | | **Total MVAGS** | | | **$600K** |
## 10.3 Research Programme
**Active Research Tracks:**
| Track | Question | Method | Timeline | Budget | |-------|----------|--------|----------|--------| | R1: Scalable Oversight | How do we maintain human oversight as AI systems exceed human capability in specific domains? | Recursive reward modelling + debate protocols | 24 months | $450K | | R2: Interpretability | How do we understand the internal reasoning of frontier models? | Mechanistic interpretability, probing classifiers, sparse autoencoders | Ongoing | $380K | | R3: Formal Verification | Can we formally prove safety properties of AI systems? | Verified neural network properties (Lipschitz bounds, monotonicity, fairness) | 18 months | $520K | | R4: Multi-Agent Governance | How do we govern ecosystems of interacting AI agents? | Agent-based simulation, game-theoretic analysis | 12 months | $280K | | R5: Value Alignment | How do we specify and maintain alignment with human values at scale? | Constitutional AI extensions, value learning, preference aggregation | Ongoing | $600K |
**Total Research Budget:** $2.23M over 24 months
## 10.4 International Coordination
- **Bletchley Declaration (2023):** Signatory commitment to frontier AI safety; enterprise contribution to national capability - **Seoul Frontier AI Safety Commitments (2024):** Voluntary commitments on pre-deployment testing and incident reporting - **EU AI Office engagement:** Quarterly dialogue on GPAI compliance; participation in standards development - **OECD AI Policy Observatory:** Data contribution to international AI governance benchmarking - **ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42:** Active participation in AI standards development (42001, 42005, 42006)
---

# Appendix B: Investment Summary
| Domain | Component | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Total | |--------|-----------|--------|--------|--------|-------| | Governance Platform | Sentinel v2.4 -> v3.0 | $380K | $290K | $180K | $850K | | Compliance | EU AI Act + ISO 42001 + SOC 2 | $210K | $140K | $120K | $470K | | Education | Kyaw Stack (all layers) | $120K | $80K | $60K | $260K | | Crisis Simulation | Luminous Engine Codex | $150K | $100K | $80K | $330K | | Financial Services | Omni-Sentinel | $280K | $200K | $150K | $630K | | Research | 5 research tracks | $890K | $780K | $560K | $2,230K | | Architecture | Cognitive Resonance implementation | $340K | $280K | $180K | $800K | | International | GSIIEN + coordination | $90K | $110K | $120K | $320K | | **Total** | | **$2,460K** | **$1,980K** | **$1,450K** | **$5,890K** |
**ROI Projection:** 3-year NPV $12.4M at 10% discount rate (based on regulatory penalty avoidance, operational efficiency, competitive advantage, and reputational value preservation).
